title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Sets Franchise U-Turn With Rotten Tomatoes Audience Score
text: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple achieved the highest audience score in the franchise history on Rotten Tomatoes. Audiences polled by CinemaScore gave the film an average grade of A-, and audiences surveyed by PostTrak gave it an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars, with 72% saying they would definitely recommend it. The audience score for The Bone Temple is the highest for the franchise, beating out 28 Days Later's 85%. This represents a significant positive reception from general audiences. The film demonstrated the strongest appeal to demographics of men over 25 years old. The audience enthusiasm stands in marked contrast to the theatrical box office underperformance, indicating that those who saw the film appreciated it, but the film failed to attract the broader mainstream audience needed for financial success. The critical success and positive audience reception suggest the film's problems were more in marketing and release timing than in quality.
